title: What qualities do my ideal partners share
subtitle: A journal set for defining the connections you want to attract more of.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 46 of the MONEY program. The prompts ask you to describe the qualities you look for in work connections — and to notice where you already have them.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 46 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you're building your client base, looking for collaborators, or assessing an existing working relationship.

content.text: A journal set from Day 46 of the MONEY program. The prompts are practical: they ask you to name the qualities you want in work connections, and to find evidence that those connections already exist.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
Think of a working relationship that has gone well — a client, collaborator, or partner who was a good fit. What made it work?
Write the specifics. What they were like to work with. What made the exchange feel easy.
Where do you currently have people in your world who reflect the kind of energy and alignment you want more of?
Name them. Even if it's one person.
What qualities would your ideal clients, collaborators, or partners share?
Write the list. Professional qualities, personal qualities, the way they communicate, the way they value your work.
How could you energetically match those qualities today?
Write one specific thing you could do or be today that reflects the kind of working relationship you want to attract.
Is there someone in your network right now who might be an ideal connection — a client, collaborator, or referral source you haven't reached out to yet?
Name them. Write what's stopped you. Write the first sentence of the message you could send.
Adapted from Day 46 of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
